prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains a perceptron update as a decision boundary shifting after a misclassified point is found. Show two classes in 2D, a first boundary that gets one point wrong, then move the boundary so the highlighted point lands on the correct side while a tiny hinge-loss readout drops.
completion:
from manim import *

class PerceptronBoundaryUpdateStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('A Perceptron Update Shifts The Boundary After A Mistake', font_size=26).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 6, 1], y_range=[0, 6, 1], x_length=5.0, y_length=4.8, tips=False).shift(LEFT * 2.4 + DOWN * 0.1)
        blue_pts = VGroup(*[Dot(axes.c2p(x, y), color=BLUE_D, radius=0.07) for x, y in [(1.1, 4.5), (1.6, 3.8), (2.0, 4.2), (1.9, 3.1)]])
        red_pts = VGroup(*[Dot(axes.c2p(x, y), color=RED_D, radius=0.07) for x, y in [(4.3, 1.2), (4.8, 2.0), (3.7, 1.7), (4.2, 2.7)]])
        hard_point = Dot(axes.c2p(3.1, 3.0), color=RED_D, radius=0.08)
        hard_ring = Circle(radius=0.18, color=YELLOW_D).move_to(hard_point)
        bad_line = DashedLine(axes.c2p(1.9, 5.5), axes.c2p(4.7, 0.9), color=GRAY_B, dash_length=0.16)
        good_line = DashedLine(axes.c2p(2.4, 5.5), axes.c2p(5.3, 1.2), color=GREEN_D, dash_length=0.16)
        card = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=1.9, height=1.05, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.12).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 0.3)
        loss_label = Text('hinge loss', font_size=18, color=ORANGE).move_to(card.get_center() + UP * 0.2)
        loss_before = Text('1.0', font_size=28, color=RED_D).move_to(card.get_center() + DOWN * 0.17)
        loss_after = Text('0.0', font_size=28, color=GREEN_D).move_to(card.get_center() + DOWN * 0.17)
        caption = Text('The update nudges the separating hyperplane so the misclassified point moves to the correct side.', font_size=21, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), FadeIn(blue_pts), FadeIn(red_pts), FadeIn(hard_point), run_time=2.1)
        self.play(Create(bad_line), FadeIn(hard_ring), FadeIn(card), FadeIn(loss_label), FadeIn(loss_before), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(bad_line, good_line), ReplacementTransform(loss_before, loss_after), run_time=2.3)
        self.play(FadeOut(hard_ring), FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(good_line, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.6)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains sigmoid saturation. Show the sigmoid curve with three highlighted operating points: left tail, center, and right tail. Underneath, add three gradient bars so it is obvious that the middle region has the strongest learning signal while the tails have tiny gradients.
completion:
from manim import *

class SigmoidSaturationStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Sigmoid Saturates In The Tails', font_size=29).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(x_range=[-5, 5, 2], y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.5], x_length=7.0, y_length=4.1, tips=False).shift(UP * 0.35)
        curve = axes.plot(lambda x: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x)), x_range=[-5, 5], color=BLUE_D)
        left_dot = Dot(axes.c2p(-4, 0.018), color=RED_D, radius=0.07)
        center_dot = Dot(axes.c2p(0, 0.5), color=GREEN_D, radius=0.07)
        right_dot = Dot(axes.c2p(4, 0.982), color=RED_D, radius=0.07)
        labels = VGroup(Text('tiny gradient', font_size=18, color=RED_D).next_to(left_dot, LEFT, buff=0.12), Text('largest gradient', font_size=18, color=GREEN_D).next_to(center_dot, UR, buff=0.12), Text('tiny gradient', font_size=18, color=RED_D).next_to(right_dot, RIGHT, buff=0.12))
        bars = VGroup(Rectangle(width=0.55, height=0.28, color=RED_D, fill_color=RED_D, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.55, height=1.35, color=GREEN_D, fill_color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.45), Rectangle(width=0.55, height=0.28, color=RED_D, fill_color=RED_D, fill_opacity=0.45)).arrange(RIGHT, buff=1.25, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(DOWN * 2.3)
        bar_title = Text('gradient size', font_size=18, color=WHITE).next_to(bars, UP, buff=0.14)
        connectors = VGroup(Line(left_dot.get_bottom(), bars[0].get_top(), color=GRAY_B), Line(center_dot.get_bottom(), bars[1].get_top(), color=GRAY_B), Line(right_dot.get_bottom(), bars[2].get_top(), color=GRAY_B))
        caption = Text('When activations sit in the flat tails, updates shrink. Learning is strongest near the steep center.', font_size=21, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(curve), run_time=2.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(left_dot), FadeIn(center_dot), FadeIn(right_dot), FadeIn(labels), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(Create(connectors), LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in bars], lag_ratio=0.16), FadeIn(bar_title), run_time=2.1)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(bars[1], color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains weight updates with a before-and-after matrix heatmap. Show one small weight matrix on the left, the updated matrix on the right, highlight the cells that changed most, and add a compact loss card that decreases at the same time.
completion:
from manim import *

class WeightHeatmapUpdateStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Weight Updates Change Many Parameters Together', font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        before_vals = [0.25, 0.55, 0.30, 0.62, 0.18, 0.44, 0.20, 0.48, 0.72]
        after_vals = [0.35, 0.40, 0.28, 0.74, 0.30, 0.38, 0.16, 0.36, 0.61]
        before = VGroup(*[Square(side_length=0.58, color=GRAY_B, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=v) for v in before_vals]).arrange_in_grid(3, 3, buff=0.05).shift(LEFT * 2.7 + DOWN * 0.15)
        after = VGroup(*[Square(side_length=0.58, color=GRAY_B, fill_color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=v) for v in after_vals]).arrange_in_grid(3, 3, buff=0.05).shift(RIGHT * 1.7 + DOWN * 0.15)
        labels = VGroup(Text('before', font_size=20, color=BLUE_D).next_to(before, UP, buff=0.14), Text('after update', font_size=20, color=TEAL_D).next_to(after, UP, buff=0.14))
        arrow = Arrow(before.get_right(), after.get_left(), buff=0.2, color=ORANGE)
        changed_before = VGroup(before[1], before[3], before[4])
        changed_after = VGroup(after[1], after[3], after[4])
        box_before = SurroundingRectangle(changed_before, color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)
        box_after = SurroundingRectangle(changed_after, color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)
        card = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=1.8, height=1.0, color=ORANGE, fill_color=ORANGE, fill_opacity=0.12).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 1.6)
        loss_label = Text('loss', font_size=18, color=ORANGE).move_to(card.get_center() + UP * 0.18)
        loss_a = DecimalNumber(1.48, num_decimal_places=2, font_size=28, color=RED_D).move_to(card.get_center() + DOWN * 0.16)
        loss_b = DecimalNumber(0.82, num_decimal_places=2, font_size=28, color=GREEN_D).move_to(card.get_center() + DOWN * 0.16)
        caption = Text('Backpropagation does not fix one cell at a time. It coordinates many weight changes so the loss falls together.', font_size=21, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(before), FadeIn(labels[0]), run_time=1.8)
        self.play(Create(box_before), FadeIn(card), FadeIn(loss_label), FadeIn(loss_a), run_time=1.4)
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrow), FadeIn(after), FadeIn(labels[1]), ReplacementTransform(box_before, box_after), ReplacementTransform(loss_a, loss_b), run_time=2.4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(box_after, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains vanishing gradients by comparing a shallow network with a deep network. Show gradient bars for both cases so the shallow model keeps usable signals across layers while the deep model’s earliest layers get almost nothing.
completion:
from manim import *

class VanishingGradientDepthComparisonStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('Deeper Networks Can Send Almost No Gradient To Early Layers', font_size=24).to_edge(UP)
        shallow_panel = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=3.8, height=4.7, color=GRAY_B).shift(LEFT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.1)
        deep_panel = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=3.8, height=4.7, color=GRAY_B).shift(RIGHT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.1)
        shallow_title = Text('shallow', font_size=20, color=BLUE_D).next_to(shallow_panel, UP, buff=0.12)
        deep_title = Text('deep', font_size=20, color=RED_D).next_to(deep_panel, UP, buff=0.12)
        shallow_bars = VGroup(*[Rectangle(width=0.42, height=h, color=BLUE_D, fill_color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.45) for h in [1.1, 1.0, 0.9, 0.8]]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.25, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(shallow_panel.get_center() + DOWN * 0.45)
        deep_bars = VGroup(*[Rectangle(width=0.28, height=h, color=RED_D, fill_color=RED_D, fill_opacity=0.45) for h in [0.12, 0.18, 0.26, 0.38, 0.55, 0.78, 1.05, 1.35]]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.14, aligned_edge=DOWN).move_to(deep_panel.get_center() + DOWN * 0.45)
        shallow_arrow = Arrow(shallow_panel.get_top() + DOWN * 0.55, shallow_panel.get_bottom() + UP * 1.5, buff=0.0, color=ORANGE)
        deep_arrow = Arrow(deep_panel.get_top() + DOWN * 0.55, deep_panel.get_bottom() + UP * 1.5, buff=0.0, color=ORANGE)
        notes = VGroup(Text('usable signal', font_size=18, color=BLUE_D).next_to(shallow_panel, DOWN, buff=0.14), Text('early layers starve', font_size=18, color=RED_D).next_to(deep_panel, DOWN, buff=0.14))
        caption = Text('If each layer shrinks the backward signal, very deep stacks can leave the earliest weights with almost no update.', font_size=21, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(shallow_panel), FadeIn(deep_panel), FadeIn(shallow_title), FadeIn(deep_title), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(GrowArrow(shallow_arrow), LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in shallow_bars], lag_ratio=0.14), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(GrowArrow(deep_arrow), LaggedStart(*[GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN) for bar in deep_bars], lag_ratio=0.1), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(FadeIn(notes), FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(deep_bars[0], color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.6)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 12-second Manim scene that explains why XOR needs nonlinearity. Show the XOR points in input space with one straight boundary crossed out, then transform them into a new representation on the right where a single vertical separator now works.
completion:
from manim import *

class XorNonlinearityStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text('XOR Needs A Nonlinear Transformation', font_size=28).to_edge(UP)
        left_panel = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=3.8, height=4.2, color=GRAY_B).shift(LEFT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.05)
        right_panel = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.12, width=3.8, height=4.2, color=GRAY_B).shift(RIGHT * 3.0 + DOWN * 0.05)
        left_pts = VGroup(Dot(left_panel.get_center() + LEFT * 0.75 + UP * 0.95, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08), Dot(left_panel.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.75 + DOWN * 0.95, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08), Dot(left_panel.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.75 + UP * 0.95, color=ORANGE, radius=0.08), Dot(left_panel.get_center() + LEFT * 0.75 + DOWN * 0.95, color=ORANGE, radius=0.08))
        bad_sep = DashedLine(left_panel.get_center() + LEFT * 1.2 + DOWN * 1.3, left_panel.get_center() + RIGHT * 1.2 + UP * 1.3, color=GRAY_B, dash_length=0.16)
        cross_a = Line(bad_sep.get_start() + UP * 0.2, bad_sep.get_end() + DOWN * 0.2, color=RED_D, stroke_width=5)
        cross_b = Line(bad_sep.get_start() + DOWN * 0.2, bad_sep.get_end() + UP * 0.2, color=RED_D, stroke_width=5)
        right_pts = VGroup(Dot(right_panel.get_center() + LEFT * 0.85 + UP * 0.55, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08), Dot(right_panel.get_center() + LEFT * 0.85 + DOWN * 0.55, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08), Dot(right_panel.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.85 + UP * 0.45, color=ORANGE, radius=0.08), Dot(right_panel.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.85 + DOWN * 0.45, color=ORANGE, radius=0.08))
        good_sep = DashedLine(right_panel.get_center() + UP * 1.35, right_panel.get_center() + DOWN * 1.35, color=GREEN_D, dash_length=0.16)
        arrow = Arrow(left_panel.get_right(), right_panel.get_left(), buff=0.16, color=YELLOW_D)
        titles = VGroup(Text('input space', font_size=18, color=WHITE).next_to(left_panel, UP, buff=0.12), Text('hidden representation', font_size=18, color=WHITE).next_to(right_panel, UP, buff=0.12))
        caption = Text('One straight line fails on XOR, but a nonlinear hidden representation can rearrange the points until a simple separator works.', font_size=20, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_group = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.88, buff=0.14), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(left_panel), FadeIn(right_panel), FadeIn(titles), FadeIn(left_pts), run_time=2.0)
        self.play(Create(bad_sep), Create(cross_a), Create(cross_b), run_time=1.5)
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrow), LaggedStart(*[TransformFromCopy(a, b) for a, b in zip(left_pts, right_pts)], lag_ratio=0.12), run_time=2.1)
        self.play(Create(good_sep), FadeIn(caption_group), Indicate(good_sep, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=1.6)
        self.wait(1)
